summary_one_line: A well-formed font that shapes a multi-glyph buffer where some glyph carries HB_GLYPH_FLAG_UNSAFE_TO_BREAK so the line-124 mask test goes true.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally valid sfnt/font input (consistent table header at offsets 0x00-0x0f, e.g. the '01 46 ... kern' shape) that shapes into multiple glyphs and sets HB_GLYPH_FLAG_UNSAFE_TO_BREAK on at least one glyph, making info[end-(forward?0:1)].mask & HB_GLYPH_FLAG_UNSAFE_TO_BREAK true at line 124.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winner reaches line 124 with T=540 F=0 (the OR-arm true side fires), and value_profile's CMP_MAP distance buckets reward inputs whose CMP operands approach matching the font-table magic/length checks, steering the corpus toward valid sfnt headers that survive shaping; winning seeds carry the consistent '01 ... kern' header at offsets 0x00-0x0f.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks 8/10 trials and at line 124 reaches only T=0 F=180 (mask bit never set); loser seeds are short/garbled (6-byte and 35-byte inputs with no coherent table header) so they never shape into a buffer whose glyphs carry the UNSAFE_TO_BREAK flag.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's per-CMP distance-bucket feedback (CMP_MAP gradient) gives partial credit for getting closer to satisfying the font-format integer comparisons, so it climbs toward valid headers that produce shaped buffers with UNSAFE_TO_BREAK glyphs; naive has no such gradient and stalls on the all-or-nothing checks.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same program-side requirement: a valid font that shapes a multi-glyph buffer with an UNSAFE_TO_BREAK glyph so line 124's mask test is true.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: minimizer resolves 9/10 with the lowest avg blocked duration (6.00h) and the highest branch hitcount (3221); its calibration-driven power schedule grants smaller/faster/more-stable seeds more havoc energy, and the winning seeds are compact valid font headers (e.g. the 99-byte and 133-byte 'kern' inputs) that get heavily mutated into reaching the true side.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive runs a plain StdMutationalStage with no calibrated energy, blocks 8/10, and stays at T=0 F=180 on line 124; its loser seeds are unstructured fragments that the uniform mutation budget never refines into a valid shaping input.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: minimizer's CalibrationStage + StdPowerMutationalStage (calibrated energy) concentrates mutation effort on small stable valid-font seeds, accelerating discovery of an input that shapes a buffer with an UNSAFE_TO_BREAK glyph; naive's flat energy schedule does not.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same program-side requirement: a valid font shaping a multi-glyph buffer with an UNSAFE_TO_BREAK glyph that makes line 124's mask test true.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 8/10 with branch hitcount 2615; its CtxHook folds the call-context into the edge-map index so reaching the line-124/shaping code via the recursive hb_shape_full -> verify -> buffer_verify_unsafe_to_break path counts as new (call-context, edge) coverage, retaining intermediate inputs that drive deeper into the verify machinery.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: plain naive collapses these distinct call paths onto the same bare edges, blocks 8/10, and never sets the mask true (line 124 T=0 F=180); its loser seeds are short and never re-enter the shaping path with the context diversity that would expose the UNSAFE_TO_BREAK glyph state.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's context-sensitive coverage (CtxHook (call-context, edge) pairs) preserves inputs that reach the deep recursive shaping/verify path, giving extra corpus retention that naive's bare-edge feedback lacks, so the winner reaches the line-124 true side.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of an OR-arm mask test at line 124 inside buffer_verify_unsafe_to_break.
The globally blocked side is the true branch.
On line 124 the loser never takes the true side while the winner does.
value_profile wins over naive on subject 12 with p_AUC=0.0002.
value_profile uses a CMP_MAP keyed by operand-distance bucket giving partial credit for near-matching comparisons.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
each CMP callback writes into a `CMP_MAP` keyed by (PC, operand-distance
bucket)

evidence_trail.claim: Winning seeds carry a consistent font header with a 'kern' tag at offsets 0x00-0x0f.
minimizer wins over naive on subject 18 by calibrated energy with the lowest blocked duration and highest hitcount.
minimizer derives havoc energy from a calibration-based perf_score favoring faster, smaller, more-stable seeds.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
faster, smaller,
more-stable seeds earn more mutations

evidence_trail.claim: minimizer's winning seeds are compact valid font inputs found by minimizer trial 1.
naive_ctx wins over naive on subject 15 by context coverage.
naive_ctx folds call context into the edge-map index so a new (call-context, edge) pair is new coverage.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge

evidence_trail.claim: The shaping/verify path is reached recursively via hb_shape_full calling verify.
naive blocks the branch in 8 of 10 trials and is the loser in all three pairs.
Loser seeds are short, unstructured fragments such as a 6-byte input found by naive.
naive has no I2S or CMP distance feedback and runs a plain mutational stage.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a new bucket triggers a
corpus-add (LibAFL `MaxMapFeedback` over the edge map)

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: multi_feature branch: the value_profile>naive pair is attributed to CMP_MAP gradient (not I2SRandReplace), and the minimizer and naive_ctx pairs to calibrated energy and context-sensitive coverage respectively, so no I2SRandReplace lineage check is mandated; lineage was not run because none of the decisive deltas is cmplog/vpc input-to-state substitution.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the line-124 true side fires on inputs that do NOT require a valid shaped font buffer (e.g. a trivial CMP gate with no glyph/shaping dependency) and naive still resolves it as fast as value_profile/minimizer/naive_ctx, showing no font-structural-validity requirement and no technique-specific advantage.

weakest_evidence_point: The shared program-side requirement (a glyph actually carrying HB_GLYPH_FLAG_UNSAFE_TO_BREAK) is inferred from source plus W/L line-124 divergence; the byte diff shows valid-vs-garbled headers but does not directly prove which bytes set the UNSAFE_TO_BREAK flag.
